channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The adorable BDX Droids are coming to more Disney Parks around the world, including Disney World
item.description: Disney Parks is bringing the adorable and impressive 'BDX Droids' to more parks worldwide, including Disney World. Plus, more details were unveiled for the update on the Smugglers Run attraction at Galaxy's Edge.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: iPad shoppers beware: One of the new models is not like the others
item.description: This week, Apple updated half of its iPad lineup. After updating the iPad Pro and iPad mini in 2024, the company has just unveiled a third-generation iPad Air and an eleventh-generation iPad.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: This extraordinary rugged laptop can take up to three batteries and three SSDs and even comes with an Nvidia GPU
item.description: Getac's B360 series offers AI tools, triple battery support and Nvidia GPU, setting new standards for extreme environments.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot lands world's first front flip
item.description: A robotics firm in China claims a robot has performed the world's first humanoid robot front flip, which is significantly more difficult than a backflip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deepfake scammers are hijacking TikTok’s wellness craze to sell dubious health products
item.description: By now, most people know not to trust everything they see on TikTok. But scams on the platform are becoming increasingly sophisticated, thanks to deepfake technology.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: February’s IT job market in the US shows mixed signals; unemployment ticks up
item.description: “Sluggish” might be the best word to describe the IT job market after the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published its data for February employment today. The data offered mixed signals for US tech jobs: even though the number of tech roles grew, unemployment rose.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride
item.description: This blog will focus on requirements in the context of the federal government. Sound requirements drive best value pricing and performance for customer agencies The post Driving best value pricing – Let’s go for a ride first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk's SpaceX confirms Starlink lease agreement with FAA
item.description: SpaceX confirmed Wednesday that it is leasing Starlink kits and service to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for free but denied any push to take over Verizon’s contract to build an updated communications system for the agency.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Out of the lab and into the streets, researchers and doctors rally for science against Trump cuts
item.description: Researchers, doctors, their patients and supporters are venturing out of labs, hospitals and offices across the country to stand up to what they call an attack on life-saving science by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US military's mini space shuttle returns to Earth after orbiting for 434 days on a secret mission
item.description: The U.S. military's classified mini space shuttle has returned to Earth after circling the world for 434 days.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Private lunar lander is declared dead after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon's south pole.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Siete referentes de liderazgo femenino en TI para el 8M
item.description: Pese a los importantes avances en materia de igualdad entre hombres y mujeres que se han logrado en los últimos años, el de la equidad es un camino al que aún le queda recorrido. También en el sector tecnológico, en el que aún persiste cierta segregación por género.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why weird JD Vance memes have taken over the internet
item.description: Ironically enough, a divisive moment in the Oval Office last weekend seems to have brought the entire internet together.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order Thursday to create a government reserve of bitcoin along with a "digital asset stockpile" in the administration's latest embrace of the cryptocurrency industry. According to the order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Gig Companies are backing Trump’s Labor Secretary nominee. Here’s what that means for workers
item.description: The trade association representing America’s largest gig companies is backing President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Labor—an endorsement that could shape the future of worker classification in the gig economy.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Alibaba says its new AI model rivals DeepSeeks’s R-1, OpenAI’s o1
item.description: Alibaba Cloud on Thursday launched QwQ-32B, a compact reasoning model built on its latest large language model (LLM), Qwen2.5-32b, one it says delivers performance comparable to other large cutting edge models, including Chinese rival DeepSeek and OpenAI’s o1, with only 32 billion parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.98 shines on GitHub Copilot
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.98 has arrived, featuring enhancements to the GitHub Copilot AI coding assistant, including previews of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, Copilot agent mode improvements, and Copilot Edits support for notebook files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk’s Starlink could soon tap into $42 billion federal program
item.description: The Commerce Department has changed its rules in a way that could open the door for Starlink, Elon Musk’s satellite internet service, to become part of a federal $42 billion grant program to bring high-speed service to rural and poor areas of the United States.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Social Security Administration bans reading 'general news' at work
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) has banned reading “general news” websites at work, The Hill has learned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats ask for investigation into whether Musk is using government position to influence X advertisers
item.description: Democratic senators are asking the Department of Justice (DOJ) to investigate whether tech billionaire Elon Musk, a senior adviser to President Trump, is leveraging his government position to pressure advertisers into spending more on his social media platform, X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Senate Banking Committee Chair Tim Scott (R-S.C.) unveiled legislation Thursday taking aim at debanking.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City, rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Judiciary panel subpoenas Google, YouTube parent company
item.description: House Judiciary Committee Chair Jim Jordan (R-Ohio) on Thursday subpoenaed Alphabet, the parent company of Google and YouTube, demanding the tech giant turn over its communications with the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: LibreOffice downloads on the rise as users look to avoid subscription costs
item.description: Interest in LibreOffice, the open-source alternative to Microsoft Office, is on the rise, with weekly downloads of its software package close to 1 million a week. That’s the highest download number since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Trump administration just cut Defense Department grants that research terrorism and drug trafficking
item.description: Researchers in a highly regarded Department of Defense program called the Minerva Research Initiative recently received word that grants already awarded by the Defense Department are being terminated, potentially putting their work—and in some cases even paychecks for their students and lab staff—in
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Seven important trends in the server sphere
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: How Audiomack became an unlikely Spotify competitor
item.description: Kendrick Lamar. Drake. Lady Gaga. The charts of music streaming services pretty much all look the same these days, with familiar names dominating the top spots—except on up-and-coming Spotify competitor Audiomack. The current No.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Without being there, Apple hits Mobile World Congress
item.description: If you overlook the introduction of the iPhone 16e with its own Apple-designed 5G modem along with the release of new iPads, Macs and processors, you might argue that Apple didn’t make an impression at this year’s Mobile World Congress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Employee screening data breach exposes 3.3 million records
item.description: Major employee screening company data breach affects 3.3 million people. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and gives five ways to protect your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
